sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To start an outage simulation, you must have
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to
the project.

sections.heading: Simulate Regional Outage Process
sections.body:
When you submit a request to test an outage using the Atlas UI
or API (Application Programming Interface), Atlas simulates an outage event. During a simulated
outage, Atlas:

- Removes network connectivity to nodes in the selected regions.

- Does **not** trigger a monitoring alert for [Replica set has no primary](<Replica set has no primary>).

- Automatically ends the simulation after a configurable expiration period (1, 3, or 7 days).

If your application takes more than 15 minutes to notice connection loss
to some nodes, we recommend that you reduce your TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) retransmission
timeout values. To learn more, see [modify tcp\_retries2 value](manual/faq/diagnostics).

sections.heading: Simulate Regional Outage Using the Atlas UI
sections.body: To simulate a Regional Outage in the Atlas UI:

sections.heading: Step 1: Go to the  modal.
sections.body:
- For the cluster you wish to perform outage testing, click
  the **...** button.

- Click **Test Resilience**.

- Select **Regional Outage**. Atlas displays a
  **Test Resilience** modal with the steps Atlas
  takes to simulate an outage event. To learn more, see
  [Simulate Regional Outage Process](test-outage-process).

sections.heading: Step 2: Begin the simulation.
sections.body:
- Click **Select Regions**.

- Select the tab corresponding to the type of outage you want to simulate:

  **Minority:**

  Minority of Electable Nodes

  Select fewer than half of your electable nodes.

  **Majority:**

  Majority of Electable Nodes

  Select at least one more than half of your electable
  nodes and keep at least one electable node remaining.

  After selecting a majority of your electable nodes, your
  replica set won't have a [primary node](manual/core/replica-set-members).
  This means that your replica set can't perform write
  operations and read operations that are not configured
  with a suitable [readPreference](manual/core/read-preference).

- (Optional) From the **Simulation Duration** dropdown, select the
  duration for the simulation to run before it automatically expires.
  This value defaults to `3` days.

- Select **Simulate Regional Outage** to begin the test.

  Atlas notifies you when the outage occurs.

sections.heading: Step 3: End the simulation.
sections.body:
The simulation ends automatically once the duration set for the **Simulation Duration** is reached. You can also manually end the simulation earlier.> **Note:** Atlas checks for expired simulations in 24-hour intervals so it may take
> up to an additional day after the expiration date for the simulation to fully
> resolve.Select a tab corresponding to the type of outage you are
performing:**Minority:**Minority of Electable NodesWhen you finish testing the outage, click
**End Simulation**.**Majority:**Majority of Electable NodesWhen you finish testing the regional outage, you can perform
one of the following:* Restore your multi-region cluster by adding
  electable nodes to your replica set. To learn more, see
  [Reconfigure a Replica Set During a Regional Outage](reconfigure-rs-during-regional-outage).

* End the regional outage simulation by clicking
  **End Simulation**.

sections.heading: Simulate Regional Outage Using the API (Application Programming Interface)
sections.body:
You can use the [Test Outage](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-startgroupclusteroutagesimulation)
API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint to simulate an outage event. To learn more about the
outage process, see [Simulate Regional Outage Process](test-outage-process).

sections.heading: Verify the Outage
sections.body:
To verify that the outage is successful, monitor your application and
ensure your read and write operations are working as expected.

sections.heading: Troubleshoot Outage
sections.body:
A regional outage or regional outage simulation that affects the
highest priority regions in a sharded cluster could cause the
cluster to become inoperable for read operations. To restore the config
servers, do the following:

- Configure a [read preference](manual/core/read-preference) that is
  suitable for querying secondary nodes for reads.

- [Reconfigure a Replica Set During a Regional Outage](reconfigure-rs-during-regional-outage) the
  cluster for regaining electable nodes.
